name: Revenue Development Area of Federal Way
description:
Federal Way's revenue development area boundary.To participate in the LIFT program, local jurisdictions
establish a revenue development area (RDA) and apply to the Community Economic
Revitalization Board (CERB) for approval. There are currently nine LIFT
projects around the state; the program is closed to further projects unless the
Legislature increases the total amount of funding available for state
contributions. The amount of state contribution each project receives per year
is limited to th

name: Regional Growth Center of Federal Way
description: Regional Growth Centers (RGC) are locations of more compact, pedestrian-oriented development with a mix of housing, jobs, retail, services, and other destinations. Federal Way’s nomination of the City Center-Core (CC-C) zoning area as boundary for Regional Growth Center was confirmed in 1994. Planning for the RGC is embedded in the Chapter 7 – City Center, of the City’s Comprehensive Plan. More information can be found here: https://engagefw.com/downtown.

name: Regional Growth Center of Federal Way
description: Regional Growth Centers (RGC) are locations of more compact, pedestrian-oriented development with a mix of housing, jobs, retail, services, and other destinations. Federal Way’s nomination of the City Center-Core (CC-C) zoning area as boundary for Regional Growth Center was confirmed in 1994. Planning for the RGC is embedded in the Chapter 7 – City Center, of the City’s Comprehensive Plan. More information can be found here: https://engagefw.com/downtown.

name: Downtown SEPA Area of Federal Way
description: Under the State Environmental Policy Act (SEPA), a planned action ordinance is a way to have an environmental review of a specific geographic area before any permits have been filed. The City of Federal Way conducted an Environmental Impact Statement (EIS) in 2006 covering development in the commercial, residential, lodging, office, and institutional sectors through the end of 2014. A Supplemental EIS (SEIS) was completed in 2015, which analyzes residential, commercial, office, hotel, and other

name: Comprehensive Plan of Federal Way
description: This layer contains the 2024 comprehensive zoning plan for the city of Federal Way. A comprehensive plan is required by the state of Washington every 10 years. It conveys the goals, objectives, policies, actions, and standards that will guide the city's elected representatives and government staff in making decisions. The plan must include elements on land use, housing, capital facilities plan, utilities, and transportation; it can also include sections on economic development, parks and recreat

name: New Development Projects Dashboard
description:
The New Projects Dashboard can be used 
by the community to stay up to date with the current Community 
Development projects in the city. Includes projects that are 
planned/approved, under review, and under construction. Developments can
 come from many categories - commercial, residential/housing, schools, 
faith-based facilities, and utilities.
